title: A Lieutenant General Sings
content:
I am the Division Commander,
    His Excellency.
    I have attained what is humanly possible.
    A lovely consciousness.
    In front of me
    Important people and chiefs of regiments
    Bend their knees,
    And my generals
    Obey my commands.
    God willing, my next command will be
    An entire military corps.
    Women, drama, music
    Do not interest me much.
    Compared to parades and battles,
    That does not amount to much.
    Would that there were an endless war
    With bloody, howling winds.
    Ordinary life
    Has no charm for me.
meaning:
Alfred Lichtenstein's contribution to classic is further solidified by the brilliance found in "A Lieutenant General Sings"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
